mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or signal loses predictive power during stable employment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production contracts for two consecutive months while inflation remains elevated, supply-side bottlenecks compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive during concurrent industrial contraction and high inflation, or the mechanism fails to predict direction in > 50% of stagflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real purchasing power and prompt expectations of tighter monetary policy, generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns as discount rates adjust upward.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show positive correlation with CPIAUCSL increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate reflects a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55% of observations, or interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a sharp rebound in real GDP growth signals a post-recession recovery phase, prompting aggressive equity positioning and positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 turns positive after 2 consecutive quarters of decline
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain flat or negative following confirmed GDP rebounds, or the signal loses predictive accuracy in > 60% of recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indices reflect improving manufacturing activity and supply chain normalization, reducing input cost pressures and generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with industrial production growth.
